title: nymphaea stellata f. caerulea
intro: Nymphaea stellata f. caerulea, also known as the blue water lily or blue lotus, is a subspecies of aquatic plant belonging to the genus Nymphaea. This plant has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The blue water lily is believed to have health benefits such as reducing anxiety and improving cognitive function, as well as possessing potential anti-cancer properties. Herbal preparations of Nymphaea stellata f. caerulea include dried flowers and roots, which can be used to make teas, infusions, and extracts for medicinal purposes.
